tool_name: CPM Schedule Delay, Liquidated Damages & Acceleration (Crashing) Analysis

inputs.name: Excusable Delay — Force Majeure, Client-Caused
inputs.hint: Delays entitling EOT (Extension of Time) under contract — weather, client instruction, force majeure

inputs.name: Compensable Delay — Client-Caused, Recoverable
inputs.hint: Client-caused delays for which contractor may claim additional cost (prolongation cost)

inputs.name: Liquidated Damages Rate
inputs.hint: From contract clause — pre-agreed LD rate per day of non-excusable delay
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: LD Cap (% of Contract Value)
inputs.hint: Most contracts cap LD at 5–15% of contract value — FIDIC: typically 10%

inputs.name: Total Contract Value
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Acceleration Cost per Day Recovered
inputs.hint: Overtime + additional resources + expediting — marginal cost to compress 1 critical path day
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Maximum Compressible Days (Critical Path Limit)
inputs.hint: Physical limit of schedule compression — beyond this, additional resources yield no time saving
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Contractor Prolongation Cost (site overhead/day)
inputs.hint: Site overhead rate per day: site manager, equipment standing, temporary facilities
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Disruption / Productivity Loss Cost
inputs.hint: Loss of productivity from concurrent delays — Measured Mile or Earned Value method
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

formulas: Gross_Delay_days = MAX(0, forecast_duration_days - planned_duration_days)   // [days] Total schedule overrun
NonExcusable_Delay_days = MAX(0, Gross_Delay_days - excusable_delay_days - compensable_delay_days)   // [days] Contractor-culpable delay
Base_LD_Exposure_USD = NonExcusable_Delay_days * daily_ld_rate_usd   // [USD] LD before cap
LD_Cap_USD = contract_value_usd > 0 ? contract_value_usd * (ld_cap_pct / 100) : 999999999   // [USD] Maximum LD payable
Capped_LD_USD = MIN(Base_LD_Exposure_USD, LD_Cap_USD)   // [USD] LD after contractual cap
Optimal_Crash_Days = daily_ld_rate_usd > crash_cost_per_day_usd ? MIN(NonExcusable_Delay_days, max_crashable_days) : 0   // [days] Economically optimal days to crash
Total_Crash_Cost_USD = Optimal_Crash_Days * crash_cost_per_day_usd   // [USD] Total acceleration cost
Residual_LD_USD = MIN((NonExcusable_Delay_days - Optimal_Crash_Days) * daily_ld_rate_usd, LD_Cap_USD)   // [USD] LD after crashing
Net_Financial_Impact_USD = Total_Crash_Cost_USD + Residual_LD_USD   // [USD] Minimum total cost scenario
Savings_From_Crashing_USD = Capped_LD_USD - Net_Financial_Impact_USD   // [USD] Financial benefit of acceleration
Prolongation_Claim_USD = compensable_delay_days * prolongation_cost_per_day_usd + disruption_cost_usd   // [USD] Recoverable prolongation claim
Net_Contractor_Exposure_USD = Capped_LD_USD - Prolongation_Claim_USD   // [USD] Net exposure after claims offset
Crash_ROI_ratio = Total_Crash_Cost_USD > 0 ? Savings_From_Crashing_USD / Total_Crash_Cost_USD : 0   // [—] Return on acceleration investment

planned_duration_minimum.error_msg: Planned duration must be at least 1 day.

excusable_exceeds_delay.error_msg: Excusable delay cannot exceed total gross delay. Check inputs — contractor may be entitled to EOT only up to the actual overrun.

crash_exceeds_delay.error_msg: Maximum crashable days exceeds total delay. You cannot recover more time than the delay incurred.

smart_warnings.message: CRASHING NOT ECONOMICAL: Acceleration cost per day exceeds LD rate. Paying liquidated damages is financially preferable to accelerating — unless client relationship, repeat business, or reputational risk justifies the premium.

smart_warnings.source: FIDIC / Contract Law — LD Cap Reached
smart_warnings.message: LD CAP REACHED: Liquidated damages have hit the contractual maximum. Any additional delay beyond this point no longer increases LD exposure — however, client may pursue general damages if LD is found to be a penalty clause. Seek legal advice.

CRITICAL DELAY: Non-excusable overrun exceeds 20% of planned duration. This is a major contract performance failure. Formal delay analysis (TIA — Time Impact Analysis) required immediately. Consider formal acceleration notice and schedule recovery plan submission.

RECOVERABLE CLAIM EXCEEDS LD: Prolongation costs attributable to client-caused delays exceed LD exposure. Net position may be a client payment obligation to contractor. Formal contractual notice and substantiation records are essential.
SCHEDULE CANNOT BE RECOVERED: No crashable days available on critical path. Full LD exposure is unavoidable. Focus on EOT entitlement claims, LD cap, and dispute avoidance strategy.
HIGH CRASH ROI (>3×): Acceleration investment yields more than 3× return in LD savings. Full crash programme to maximum compressible days is strongly recommended on financial grounds alone.

fmea.effect: Excusable delay cannot exceed total gross delay. Check inputs — contractor may be entitled to EOT only up to the actual overrun.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Maximum crashable days exceeds total delay. You cannot recover more time than the delay incurred.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: CRASHING NOT ECONOMICAL: Acceleration cost per day exceeds LD rate. Paying liquidated damages is financially preferable to accelerating — unless client relationship, repeat business, or reputational risk justifies the premium.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: crash_cost_per_day_usd > daily_ld_rate_usd

fmea.failureMode: LD CAP REACHED: Liquidated damages have hit the contractual maximum. Any additional delay beyond this point no longer increases LD exposure — however, client may pursue general damages if LD is found to be a penalty clause. Seek legal advice.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Base_LD_Exposure_USD >= LD_Cap_USD && LD_Cap_USD > 0
fmea.control_measure: FIDIC / Contract Law — LD Cap Reached

fmea.failureMode: CRITICAL DELAY: Non-excusable overrun exceeds 20% of planned duration. This is a major contract performance failure. Formal delay analysis (TIA — Time Impact Analysis) required immediately. Consider formal acceleration notice and schedule recovery plan submission.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: NonExcusable_Delay_days > planned_duration_days * 0.2

fmea.failureMode: RECOVERABLE CLAIM EXCEEDS LD: Prolongation costs attributable to client-caused delays exceed LD exposure. Net position may be a client payment obligation to contractor. Formal contractual notice and substantiation records are essential.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Prolongation_Claim_USD > Capped_LD_USD && compensable_delay_days > 0

fmea.failureMode: SCHEDULE CANNOT BE RECOVERED: No crashable days available on critical path. Full LD exposure is unavoidable. Focus on EOT entitlement claims, LD cap, and dispute avoidance strategy.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Gross_Delay_days > 0 && max_crashable_days === 0

fmea.failureMode: HIGH CRASH ROI (>3×): Acceleration investment yields more than 3× return in LD savings. Full crash programme to maximum compressible days is strongly recommended on financial grounds alone.
